review_title: Easy way to share visual issues
description: This is the kind of software that saves you time allowing you to focus on what really matter.
author: Alex G.
pros: As a developer, I love that all issues reported into Jira actually come with the source URL and information about the user environment (Browser, OS, screen size, ...)
cons: I really hope that the team at Marker.io will build the ability to edit the built-in bug reporting template

review_title: Does a few, specific things *extremely* well.
author: Ben S.
pros: Marker saves our team tons of time by allowing our clients to report bugs and issues on the software we developer quickly and efficiently. Clients feel empowered to be able to send feedback with zero friction or issues. The resulting tickets go directly into Jira so we can prioritize alongside all of our other work. Marker is perfect for what it sets out to do.Also - their support is fantastic. The [sensitive content hidden] is responsive and takes care to act on feedback and feature requests super quickly.
cons: N/A - it is perfect for what it aims to do.

review_title: Marker is great for some things but not others
description: Very nice interface and integrations. May be a bit too expensive for a single web designer/developer. Would like to see on-page integration.
author: Christian O.
pros: I tried Marker for QA and issue tracking on websites. It works great to create tickets and integrates nicely with Trello (and many other Kanban-style software). There is a nice integration for allowing users to post their feedback either as a guest or a registered user.
cons: Marker does not have real-time live issue tracking for website projects. This was crucial for me so that there were no duplicate tickets on a single page. There are probably workarounds but I wanted something that would allow me to navigate to a webpage and see the tickets associated with it directly on the page.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Bug reporting has never been easier since I've been using Marker !!
author: Vincent L.
pros: Before Marker, it was very difficult to explain a bug to my development team. I had to take print screens, then send by email, explain in the email the bug, etc. Thanks to Marker, it became very easy! all done in <10 seconds!
cons: It would really be nice if the team could add video recording as a new feature to capture more dynamic bugs

review_title: Premium capture and annotate tool built for Agencies
author: Sean H.
pros: Marker.io makes it easy for our clients, project managers, QA and designers to report bugs and issues to our development team via Trello and other support services we employ.
cons: If Marker.io could grab the content of the console log, that would be awesome. Also, really looking forward to having access the Browserstack integration!

review_title: Super simple way to report web app issues to my team
description: Saved a bunch of time and it was a really easy setup with the Chrome (and now Firefox) extension.
author: Thomas K.
pros: GitHub integration was the big winner for me. As soon as I spot a bug in our web app, I report a GitHub issue without having to log into the GitHub interface. It's a big time-saver for me.
cons: I'd love for the ability to create and edit different bug report templates for separate GitHub repositories. This would be really helpful and save me even more time :)

review_title: Reduced our QA process time by 60%
author: David D.
pros: Marker.io is all about reducing the number of steps and it has helped us reduce our QA process time by more than 60%.
cons: Can't wait for the Browserstack integration to get out of beta so that all issues reported via Marker.io will include the meta data of the Browserstack device

review_title: It just works perfectly!
author: Vincenzo R.
pros: Very easy to use visual tool. I use it all the time to explain things to customers or to create documentation for our knowledge base.
cons: I would like to be able to capture screenshot outside of webpages. A Mac app would be a great improvement!

review_title: Huge time saver!
author: Sebastien D.
pros: t's really impressive! I had tried hundreds of similar tools and no one compares with Marker.io. Huge improvement to my workflow.
cons: Maybe the only thing would be the ability to capture the console logs when I report a bug.

review_title: Simple way to share product feedback
description: Ease of use...screenshot commenting, integration with Trello, daily usage
author: Jean-Marc P.
pros: I initially signed up for the Jira integration but the Marker.io is much more than that. I use Marker.io as soon as I need to get a point across
cons: I think the pricing is a bit steep, especially for small teams but once you've started using it, it's hard to stop paying.

review_title: Reporting GitHub issues is now a pleasure
description: Win time and be loved by your dev team.
author: Andrea B.
pros: As an account manager, I'm constantly in touch with our customer who report improvements ideas and small bugs. Marker.io makes is easy for me to log that for our development team
cons: A desktop application to capture feedback outside of the browser window would be awesome, especially for desktop-based apps.

review_title: Best & first bug reporting app to be adopted across all departments
description: Creating bug reports really easy and in a consistent manner across the whole company.
author: Steffen B.
pros: It's ease of use and simplicity. - While we started using this with Trello, we have switch to Productboard for our main PM-tool. - No problem for Marker - It's just send the bug report/screenshot to the inbox-email instead
cons: It' limited to the browser only. We sometimes need to capture stuff outside the browser. - And video recording would be a big plus as well.

review_title: I use marker io on a daily basis. It has definitely made posting github issues easier
author: Steven L.
pros: The github/ slack integration is by far the most useful portion. Being able to directly create github issues had improved our efficiency
cons: Not being able to add github labels. The github repo list can be slow -ish to load. I wish there was improved slack integration

review_title: An amazing and easy to use product for web page updates
description: I enjoy finding new software tools that work. This one was definitely easy, to use, intuitive, with a smooth and easy work flow.
author: John L.
pros: On a recent web update page project, I use Marker.IO to easily capture screen shots to collaborate with my team. The integration for Trello made the workflow so easy and by assigning my team the tasks with deadlines.
cons: I think they fixed the ability to be cross web browser compatible so if anything, its even more impressive and easy to use. The only thing I can think of is to add more API tools for integration

review_title: Simplifies bug tracking via Trello
description: Productivity, Ease of Use and collaboration.
author: Patrick O.
pros: Ease of use and how I can draw on the screenshots and add additional comments before submitting to Trello. This process makes it quick to understand what an issue is and how to correct work on it from a team perspective.
cons: Wish it worked with Firefox browser. Currently using it in Chrome and be nice to use in other browsers
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: My overall experience with marker.io has been good.
author: Ifat S.
pros: Works well. The customer support is highly responsive. I had a video chat with a couple of your representatives and it was great!
cons: I'm used to using Bonfire (which is now Jira Capture) and I'm missing a few features, like I mentioned in the video chat I had with you guys (the template management feature).
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Excellent - such a useful tool for web development work and card generation for Trello.
description: It has made communication with the design and development agencies we work with much easier and massively more efficient.
author: Richard C.
pros: Easy Trello card generation, with clearly labelled/drawn on screenshots - this is immensely valuable when working with agencies. The wide variety of drawing tools and ability to write detailed notes & instructions is better than any other screenshot tool I've used.
cons: Sometimes some of the drawing tools don't behave properly; the text tool is the worst, sometimes the cursor ends up miles away from where the text is actually appearing! Would love a straight line drawing tool too, as opposed to the current freehand tool.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Amazing UX. We all love Marker.io at Sutori.
description: It saved us a lot of time during QA.
author: Yoran B.
pros: I love how easy for my team to send me bugs and feedback directly into our GitHub issue tracker. The meta data that comes with each screenshot makes it super easy for me to understand and reproduce issues.
cons: I really wish it was possible for Marker.io to capture the console logs to help me understand errors.
author_industry: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: We use Marker.io to point out bugs and suggest enhancements in our apps
author: Flavien A.
pros: The integration with Trello to organise our review and the web browser app to use directly in our platform
cons: Sometime it is not so easy to explain the problem without hiding stuff and colours are not contrasted enough
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easy to capture and send to slack!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Integrates with Slack to make project QA a breeze. Marking up screenshots for client support saves so much time.
cons: No current way to capture a hover or dropdown effect. This is available with some desktop applications that allow you to delay the capture for a few seconds so you can click or hover over an element.

review_title: We found it easy to start using marker.io and now it's an important part of our QA process.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Collecting product feedback and logging bugs visually with marker.io and a trello integration has proven to be really useful for the team. It is especially useful gathering feedback from business users on the team who may not have experience communicating about product features or bugs; the ability to mark up a screenshot removes the ambiguity that usually arises in these flows. The light-weight, visual and integrated nature of marker.io is perfect for our team.

review_title: Perfect for QA & product feedback
description: This product saves us so much time, I recommend it daily to users!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Marker.io is a key tool for our development team. I love how they completely reinvented bug tracking via deep integrations.
cons: The ability to record video is the one feature that I miss the most as sometimes bugs are easier to explain with a short Gifs

review_title: Nice product and helpfull staff
description: I used it only for 2 weeks, but the experience is as good that I subscribed for a year. I think it is expensive, but the time saved using these bug reports will make up for it.
author: Seb V.
pros: When customers file bug-reports the usually don't give the right context. With marker I have a screenshot with the possibility to draw / write on and i have very useful metadata (OS / browser / size / url /reporter etc)My client uses Jira, and the 2-way connection with Jira is very welcome!
cons: I miss the feature to sync comments trough a integration (jira)
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Using marker.io alongside Trello for project managements has streamlined our bug tracking
author: Matthew W.
pros: I love how simple it is to create a screenshot, markup the image to point out the issue and the fact that it supplies additional information such as the users browser version, resolution etc.
cons: It was a bit difficult to figure out how to delete screenshots at first, besides that its done everything I've needed it to!

review_title: Gets the job done and saves time!
description: Makes reporting issues vastly simpler and more consistent
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy visual capture enriched with critical environmental details Encourages consistency, but allows for flexible and fast reporting Integrates with backend project management systems
cons: No option to set up integrations at the company level - leaves the integration up to each user Lacks workflow sophistication - integration with backend systems can feel very open-ended
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
